_comment: csSOL-WT (withdraw ticket) collateral reserve. Underlying = csSOL queued for unstaking via Jito Vault, redeemable for wSOL after the Jito vault's epoch unlock window. Configured identically to csSOL inside elevation group 2 (90% LTV, 92% liquidation threshold via the group override) so atomic collateral swaps csSOL ↔ csSOL-WT preserve LTV. The deterrent against adversarial epoch-buying is oracle-side: csSOL-WT price = min(csSOL_price * (1 - epoch_discount), backing_per_share), so if redemptions outpace maturations the receipt's mark drops and normal liquidation kicks in.
_oracle_note: csSOL-WT price = min(csSOL_price * (1 - epoch_time_discount), pool.pending_wsol_total / cssol_wt_supply * SOL_USD). Computed by a refresh-with-pool-backing variant of accrual-oracle that reads the WithdrawQueue PDA. Address below is the accrual-oracle OUTPUT account that script setup-cssol-wt-oracle.ts will create.
_flash_loan_note: borrowLimit > 0 so klend's flash_borrow path can pull liquidity from the seed; flashLoanFee = 0 (verified on-chain on csSOL + wSOL reserves). The reserve is bootstrap-seeded with treasury-minted csSOL-WT (one-time) so the very first user collateral-swap has flash liquidity available; users then flash-repay with their freshly-minted WT, rotating the seed in place.
